definitions: To choose the simplest or least challenging option, often to avoid difficulty or hard work.
To opt for a path that requires minimal effort or resistance.
examples: He always tries to take the easy route, even if it means cutting corners.
Instead of studying, she decided to take the easy route and cheat on the test.
Taking the easy route might save time now, but it could lead to problems later.
synonyms: take the path of least resistance
